solutions.solution: (e) In first step the arrow (↑) and circle (o) attached to the line moves 45°anticlockwise and other two symbol i.e., cross (×) and square moves 45° clockwise. Now, on moving from fig (b) to fig (c) i.e., second step the arrow and circle moves 90° anticlockwise and cross and square move 90° clockwise in third step i.e., moving from fig (c) to fig (d) the arrow and square moves 45° anticlockwise and clockwise, respectively and circle and cross moves 135° anti-clockwise and clockwise respectively but this rule further is not followed in next figure, so it the odd figure.

(d) If we mark the four places as

3  4

2  1

then in each step element moves one step clockwise and increases by one but at the place of 1, a new element is introduced. This rule is not followed in option (d).

(e) In consecutive figure the U-shaped element gets inverted in order as 1 in first step, 2 in second step, 3 in third and so on this rule gets violated in figure (e), so it is the odd figure among the options.

(d) From fig (a) to fig (b) the outer element of upper figure reduces in size and becomes the inner figure for the lower figure and the inner figure of upper figure is inter changed by new figure. Now, when we move from fig (b) to fig (c) the reverse procedure is followed.
(d) Figure (a) does not belong to the group as all the other figures contain same elements.
(c) In all other figures, if both the figures on right hand side are first inverted and then joined together, the figure on left hand side is obtained.
(e) In figure (e), both the shaded designs have the common base i.e., the middle line.
(b) Figure 2, 3 and 7 move clockwise direction where as figure number 1, 4, 5 and 6 move anti-clockwise direction.
(c) The central lines in each of the two figures are parallel to each other. The same property is displayed by option (c).

(b) In each figure the number of lines inside the figure is one less than the number of sides in the figure also there a line out side each figure or another way of classification is that the number of lines present is equal to the number of sides of figure.
(a) All the arrows are pointing in four different directions.
(c) The arrow and the pin attached to the central figure are pointing in the opposite directions.
(b) Figure (a) is same as figure (d) with black white reversal of regions and figure (c) is same as figure (e) with black white reversal of regions, so the odd man out is figure (b).
(c) All except figure (c) are rotated forms of the same figure.
(b) In all options except (b) the arrows are pointing towards the face sign.
(e) All option except (e) contains a set of different symbols, where as in option (e) (Ω) symbol appears twice i.e., repeated.
(c) All option except (c) contains the set of same seven symbols.
(d) Option (b) is same as option (e) arranged in reverse order and option (a) is same as option (c) arranged in reverse order, so the odd man out is option (d).
(c) Except option (d) the rest are palindromic i.e., they read the same backwards and forward.
(d) Option (a) contains the same symbol as option (c) but in reverse order. Similarly option (b) has same symbol as option (e) in reverse order, so the odd man out is option (d).
(b) Symbols in option (a) is same as in option (d) and symbols in option (c) is same as in option (e).
(d) Only option (a) contains a repetitive symbol i.e., √.
(c) Symbols in option (a) are in reverse order of symbols in option (e) and symbols in option (b) are in reverse order of symbols in option (d).
(c) All option except option (c) contain same symbols in same order although starting with different symbol but in option (c) the sequence of symbol ΔA is reversed to AΔ.
(e) Option (e) is only option which contains an arrow pointing in upward direction.
